texto: 106When I don’t have anything to read, I feel like a tortoise without a shell or a boat without an anchor. There is nothing to hide under. Nowhere to stop and rest. When I don’t have a book, there is nowhere good or interesting to be, there is nobody to care about, nothing to hope for, and nothing to puzzle over. When I do have something to read, it keeps me breathing. It’s the reward for all the other things. It’s the thing to look forward to, the reason for doing my day.” p. 177, Dime
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a E. R. Frank: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “106When I don’t have anything to read, I feel like a tortoise without a shell or a boat without a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, reading-books, there pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
